entries.definition:
s–l–s–l the word سلسلة silsilatun is classified by the philologists either under س/ل/س s–l–s, (to be smooth, easy flowing, soft, easy of manner); and under ل/س/ل/س s–l–s–l, (of water) to be sweet, palatable or easy flowing. It is not difficult to see the semantic connection between these and the derivatives meaning ‘chain’ and ‘sequence’. However, a foreign origin has been suggested for سلسلة, either from Syriac or Aramaic. Of this root, two words occur three times in the Qurʾan: سلسلة silsilatun once and سلاسل [[unclear: salāsil]] twice.
سلسلة silsilatun [n.; pl. سلاسل salāsil] chain (69:32) ثم في سلسلة ذرعها سبعون ذراعا فاسلكوه and roll him up in a chain the length of which is seventy cubits.

It is not difficult to see the semantic connection between these and the derivatives meaning ‘chain’ and ‘sequence’. However, a foreign origin has been suggested for سلسلة, either from Syriac or Aramaic. Of this root, two words occur three times in the Qurʾan: سلسلة silsilatun once and سلاسل [[unclear: salāsil]] twice.
سلسلة silsilatun [n.; pl. سلاسل salāsil] chain (69:32) ثم في سلسلة ذرعها سبعون ذراعا فاسلكوه and roll him up in a chain the length of which is seventy cubits.
